manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: diet, pipe, post, rock, pro, flock, rubber, slack, swarm.
Your opponent's team words are: test, pose, blow, pink, flat, scrub, freak, lap.
Distractor words are: raft, hitch, bank, hack, stick, firm, chase.
The kill words are: paste.

message.content:
it.CLUE: industrial
TARGETS: pipe, post, rock, rubber
message.reasoning:
We need to provide a clue and list of target words that our clue relates to. We must not use any word from any list (team, opponent, distractor, kill) or contain parts of them. Must be single-word clue. Choose to relate to as many of our words as possible while safe.

Our team's words: diet, pipe, post, rock, pro? actually "pro" is a word, flock, rubber, slack, swarm.

We must avoid any opponent/distractor/kill words: test, pose, blow, pink, flat, scrub, freak, lap, raft, hitch, bank, hack, stick, firm, chase, paste.

Clue cannot contain parts of them (like substrings?) It says cannot contain parts of them. So we should avoid any substring that is a whole word? Probably avoid any of those words as substring. Safer to choose unrelated.

We need to relate clue to some target words. For example "herd" could relate to flock and swarm? Maybe also "herd" to slack? no. "Hydro" could relate to rubber? Not sure.

Better: "construction" could relate to pipe, post, rock, slack (as slack in rope?), pro? Not.

Maybe "bunker" relates to rock, pipe? hmm.

We could aim at 3-4 words. For example "pipeline" (but pipe is a target, can't use part of target word? The clue cannot be one of words in the lists or contain parts of them—maybe "pipe" is a target, so clue cannot contain "pipe". So can't.

Maybe clue "garden" for flock (birds), swarm (insects), rubber? not.

Clue "elastic" relates to rubber, slack (elastic slack), maybe also pipe? not.

Clue "nutrition" relates to diet only.

Maybe choose "industrial" to connect pipe, rock (mining), post (construction), rubber (manufacturing). Could be ambiguous but okay.

But risk of opponent words? No.

Let's pick "industrial". Targets: pipe, post, rock, rubber.

Check: None of those target words are opponent/distractor. Clue "industrial" does not contain any of opponent words. Good.

We must format:

CLUE: industrial
TARGETS: pipe, post, rock, rubber

That's
